channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The U.S. will resume Ukraine military aid and intelligence, as Kyiv approves ceasefire
item.description: The United States said it will resume sending military aid and intelligence to Ukraine, as Ukraine agreed to a Trump administration proposal for a monthlong ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis, Michele Kelemen, and Eleanor Beardsley, MPR News

item.title: The Latest: EU targets products from GOP-led states in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the ball is in Russia's court now that Ukraine has agreed to a cease fire.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump campaigned as a protector of free speech. Critics say his actions as president threaten it
item.description: DENVER (AP) — When President Donald Trump gave his joint address to Congress last week, he boasted that in his first few weeks back in the White House he had “brought free speech back to America.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most AAPI adults don't support cutting agencies and want a focus on costs: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: A new AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll shows that most Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders do not agree with the strategy of putting entire federal agencies on the chopping block, nor are they broadly on board with mass layoffs of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US judge temporarily halts Trump plan to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston on Tuesday blocked the Trump administration's plan to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training, finding that cuts are already affecting training programs aimed at addressing a nationwide teacher shortage. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans warn Trump not to let trade war drag as he doubles down on tariff threats
item.description: Senate Republicans are warning President Trump to not let his trade war drag on as his latest escalation sent Wall Street further into turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Judges finds a huge reduction in staff and funding would be legal at African development agency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday found that removing most grants and most staff from a U.S. federal agency that invests in African small businesses would be legal. U.S.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Michael George DeSombre to represent US in East Asia and Pacific
item.description: President Donald Trump announced his nomination of Michael George DeSombre, who was nominated to serve as the next Assistant Secretary of State for East Asia and the Pacific.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Virginia’s Earle-Sears drops first ad confronting Dem opponent over migrant crime, border wall opposition
item.description: An ad from Virginia Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears plays clips of former Rep. Abigail Spanberger's comments, including her opposition to Trump's border wall, and news clippings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court asked to intervene after email tells USAID workers to destroy classified documents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A union for U.S. Agency for International Development contractors asked a federal judge Tuesday to intervene in any destruction of classified documents after an email ordered staffers to help burn and shred agency records.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Admitted Hezbollah terrorist to be deported after entering US illegally under Biden's watch
item.description: An illegal immigrant who admitted to being linked to a terrorist network is slated to be deported, the New York Post reported on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans pass bill to avert government shutdown
item.description: The House on Tuesday passed a funding bill to avert an end-of-the-week government shutdown, teeing up the measure for consideration in the Senate. The chamber cleared the continuing resolution (CR) in a largely party-line 217-213 vote, with just one Democrat — Rep.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court won't lift block on Trump's executive order attempting to end birthright citizenship
item.description: A third appellate court has upheld a block on President Donald Trump's executive order limiting birthright citizenship. The 1st Circuit Court of Appeals made the ruling Tuesday, denying a motion from Trump's legal team to immediately overturn a block issued by a federal judge in Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 senior judges, appointed by Republicans, speak out about threats against federal judiciary
item.description: Two senior federal judges, both appointed by Republican presidents, have spoken out against threats of violence and impeachment against their colleagues in the judiciary.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House GOP tees up final vote on bill to avert shutdown
item.description: House Republicans on Tuesday advanced their bill to avert an end-of-the-week government shutdown, teeing up a final vote on the legislation later in the day. The chamber voted 216-214 to adopt a rule — which governs debate on legislation — for Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) continuing resolution.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Republican-led effort to halt climate change lawsuits in Democratic-led states
item.description: The Supreme Court has rejected a lawsuit from Republican attorneys general in 19 states aimed at blocking climate change suits against the oil and gas industry from Democratic-led states, including Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: US hasn't determined who was behind cyberattack that caused outage on Musk's X
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. officials have not determined who was behind an apparent cyberattack on the social media site X that limited access to the platform for thousands of users, according to a Trump administration official familiar with the ongoing investigation into the matter.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The latest: Trump says he'll double planned tariffs on Canadian aluminum and steel
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump says he’ll double planned tariffs on Canadian aluminum and steel, sticking with a trade war despite Monday’s stock market drop. That and more of the latest with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: How the White House hired Republican political firms to launch an anti-migrant ad campaign
item.description: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security skipped a fully competitive bidding process to give two Republican-linked firms the first part of a $200 million television ad campaign that lauds President Donald Trump for his crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How reliable is the government’s economic data? Under Trump, there are real concerns
item.description: The disbanding of committees that consulted on government data — and comments from a senior official about changing how GDP is calculated — are raising alarm about the reliability of government data.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court seems intent on taking small steps in dealing with challenges to Trump's agenda
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In fewer than 500 carefully chosen and somewhat opaque words, the Supreme Court has now weighed in twice on President Donald Trump's rapid-fire efforts to remake the federal government. The justices did not give Trump's administration what it sought.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's conservatives trying to associate Carney with Trudeau
item.description: Conservatives are on the defense after the Liberal Party picked Carney as Canada's next leader, a journalist says.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: DUI punishments higher under 2 bills introduced Monday in Nevada Senate
item.description: Republicans are proposing more severe punishment for driving under the influence with two new bills introduced Monday in the Nevada Senate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Drive to delay paid family and medical leave program stalls in Minnesota House for now
item.description: The proposal would have postponed implementation by one year. The paid family and medical leave program is set to take effect in 2026. A planned vote was the only thing postponed.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Ontario slaps 25% increase on electricity exports to U.S. in response to Trump's trade war
item.description: Trump launched a new trade war last week by imposing tariffs against Washington’s three biggest trading partners, drawing immediate retaliation from Mexico, Canada and China and sending financial markets into a tailspin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump labels ex-Australian PM Malcolm Turnbull 'weak' amid spat
item.description: The president's attack on Turnbull came after comments Turnbull made to Bloomberg that Donald Trump's style is an "advantage" to China.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Adams’ approval rating reaches record low ahead of election
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – The mayor’s race is heating up just as Mayor Eric Adams’ approval rating hits a new low, according to a new poll from Quinnipiac University. A new poll shows only 20% of voters approve of how Adams is handling his post, while 67% disapprove.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Ras Baraka has highest favorability among Democratic candidates for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New polling from Fairleigh Dickinson University gave us some early insight on who voters are looking into in the race for New Jersey governor. Democratic voters weighed in on the candidates' favorability.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Tampa Bay Ukrainian refugees worried temporary legal status could be revoked
item.description: It has been three years since Russia invaded Ukraine sparking the latest deadly conflict between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas homeless camps just move when outreach comes, but proposal might change that
item.description: When homeless camps get out of control, local governments send teams to clean up. That can be a disaster, costing people what few possessions they have. But trash and unsanitary conditions are becoming a bigger priority as residents go to elected officials demanding action.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Democrats urge Lombardo to hire fired federal workers
item.description: Democratic leaders in the Nevada Assembly are urging Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to hire federal workers laid off as part of recent firings from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: San Diego Congress member pushes Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: A San Diego congressional representative is pressing the Norwegian committee who awards the prestigious Nobel Prizes to consider President Donald Trump to be the 2025 recipient of the peace prize.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
